mechanism_narrative: A higher level of the federal funds rate increases the discount rate applied to future corporate cash flows and raises debt service costs, which mechanically reduces equity valuations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the 20-day forward return following above-median FEDFUNDS levels is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) or if the Spearman rank correlation between FEDFUNDS and 20-day returns is positive over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A rapid increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over a three-month window signals deteriorating labor market conditions and falling household income, which reduces corporate revenue expectations and drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if triggered episodes produce negative 60-day returns in fewer than 55% of instances or if the average return during triggered episodes is not below the unconditional average (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The simultaneous combination of a high federal funds rate and accelerating consumer prices creates a profit squeeze via elevated input costs and restrictive financing conditions; the joint effect produces negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days beyond the independent effect of either variable alone.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the interaction term (FEDFUNDS × CPIAUCSL) does not exhibit a negative and statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in a regression for 20-day returns or if the pairwise interaction strategy underperforms the sum of single-signal strategies by more than 50 basis points annualized.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating housing starts reflect strengthening consumer and construction demand, which lifts cyclical corporate earnings; this relationship generates positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days in post-recession recovery regimes characterized by large output gaps.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if positive HOUST momentum fails to coincide with positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in at least 60% of post-recession months or if the hit rate drops below the unconditional baseline during the designated regime.

mechanism_narrative: A decline in industrial production exceeding 1 percent over a three-month window signals contracting manufacturing output and negative operating leverage, which reduces earnings expectations and generates negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if triggered episodes fail to produce negative average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) or if the signal’s information coefficient is not below -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: The joint momentum of retail sales and housing starts captures broad consumer spending strength and residential investment simultaneously; this interaction predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days because dual demand engines overwhelm idiosyncratic sector weakness.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if the interaction term (RSAFS × HOUST) does not carry a positive and significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day returns or if the long-only strategy based on joint positive momentum underperforms the S&P 500 buy-and-hold return over a 3-year backtest.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerations predict negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days when the federal funds rate is already above 4.5%, because the Federal Reserve is compelled to deliver additional restrictive shocks that reprice equities lower.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if months with FEDFUNDS > 4.5% and rising CPIAUCSL do not produce significantly negative average 20-day returns (t-test p > 0.05) compared to low-rate regimes, or if the conditional Sharpe ratio is not at least 0.3 standard deviations below the unconditional Sharpe ratio.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in retail sales exceeding 2 percent signals an abrupt contraction in consumer demand that directly impairs top-line revenues for consumer-discretionary and staple firms, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The hypothesis is rejected if triggered declines in RSAFS are followed by negative 5-day returns in fewer than 60% of episodes or if the mean 5-day return after the trigger is not significantly below zero (t-test p > 0.05).
